ddl-ryan-connor

comments.author: ddl-rliu
comments.body: Just one comment is probably sufficient, anyways either way seems fine to me.

pr_title: [DOM-52765] Handle events with exact same timestamp in execution startup monitoring

comments.author: ddl-rliu
comments.body: My first guess was that "Pulled" would be the highest priority here, but I guess there's cases where after "Pulled" status, an error can happen; therefore we make the errors as the highest priority?

comments.author: ddl-ryan-connor
comments.body: it's because the same image can be used in multiple containers. the first one can be pulled, then get an error on the second one, so erring toward showing the error.

comments.author: ddl-ryan-connor
comments.body:
i mean, there are two instances of "trying pulling" if the same image is used in two containers.
first, it could be pulled.
then the second instance starts and there is some error in the same timestamp. then the error takes precedence.

comments.author: ddl-ryan-connor
comments.body: it's a subtle issue because the second one should just be a "cache hit" event. but i dont know, there might be some error.

comments.author: ddl-rliu
comments.body: I think I agree that based on the edge case you described, this kind of tiebreak order is slightly better.

comments.author: ddl-ryan-connor
comments.body: i also think no matter what order we have, it's not possible to be right in every case -- the fact that k8s events can have the exact same ts prevents knowing (without polling k8s api) _for sure_. unsatisfying, but just have to pick the compromise we think is best

pr_title: [DOM-54518] Wiring Controllers for compute providers admin api

comments.author: dmcwhorter-ddl
comments.body:
I think we want to do 2 things here:
1. Rename `ComputeTierCreation` to something else if its used in the update/put command as well.  It's confusing because `Creation` seems like it just goes with `create`
2. I think we want to revisit the put/patch question we discussed earlier and move to a patch based update, or at least an appraoch where we only update the fields that have changed.  @fernandoacorreia pointed out that there are concurrency concerns with re-posting all of the fields on update.  For example, if 2 different requests try to update different fields at the same time one will overwrite the other's change.

comments.author: ddl-yaniv-amar
comments.body: Following... going to have to make the same changed to model deployments & I think @ddl-s-ramirezayuso might have done the same in the model api work.

comments.author: dmcwhorter-ddl
comments.body: yes that sounds right

comments.author: fernandoacorreia
comments.body:
Sorry I didn't see this earlier.

`Creation` is an action so it's not a good name for an entity in a RESTful API. It would be an appropriate name for a RPC API.

`NewComputeTier` is a better name. It indicates that is is a variation of a ComputeTier that hasn't been created yet.

comments.author: ddl-bira-ignacio
comments.body:
thought: for future us and posterity 😄 
`override def project: Project = ???` follows the same pattern as other places in the code so this is consistent with the codebase but it could be problematic since `Project` is required.
The real fix would be making project optional in the `SearchResult` trait, but that's a larger refactor and out of scope here. 
All good as-is!

comments.author: ddl-aj-rossman
comments.body: Good callout — agreed this is tech debt inherited from the trait. The optional refactor makes sense as a follow-up but is out of scope here. We've been throwing around the idea for a larger ES refactor, I will add a ticket for this

pr_title: DOM-74899 Integrate Apps into Elastic Search

comments.author: ddl-bira-ignacio
comments.body:
question: I see we're indexing on archive as well. Will this return archived apps as well?

If there isn't a unit test for this case, maybe we could add one

comments.author: ddl-aj-rossman
comments.body: Good catch! `findById` in `MongoModelProductRepository` filters out archived apps `(isArchived == false),` so getApp returns None for archived apps. However, the previous `indexAppById` would throw an `IllegalArgumentException` in that case rather than cleaning up the index entry. I've fixed this so that when getApp returns None (archived/deleted), we now call `deleteDocument` to remove it from the ES index instead.

pr_title: DOM-74899 Integrate Apps into Elastic Search

comments.author: ddl-bira-ignacio
comments.body: nit: maybe this and the log line below should be a debug level

comments.author: ddl-aj-rossman
comments.body: Fixed 👍

pr_title: DOM-74899 Integrate Apps into Elastic Search

comments.author: ddl-bira-ignacio
comments.body: question: this loads all apps into memory at once. For deployments with a large number of apps, is this a concern?

comments.author: ddl-aj-rossman
comments.body: This is a reasonable concern- at the current scale of our cloud customers, I don't see this being an issue but definitely something we should consider for our customer managed deployments. They better way would be to "stream" apps, so we'd need listApps (or a new method) to return a lazy collection, which would be a change to the AppService interface. Is it ok if I tackle this in a subsequent PR?

comments.author: ddl-bira-ignacio
comments.body: sure thing

pr_title: DOM-74899 Integrate Apps into Elastic Search

comments.author: ddl-bira-ignacio
comments.body: question: It looks like it would match everything alongside the constrained query, is it intentional? I see some of the other search methods here don't do that.

comments.author: ddl-aj-rossman
comments.body: Yes, this is intentional. The `matchAllQuery` is used as a filter subquery in `makeSearchSourceBuilder` — it's the same pattern used by `searchFeatureViewsQuery` and `searchDatasetRwQuery`. The actual search constraint comes from the `makeConstrainedQuery` call which builds the must clause with the user's search phrase. The `matchAllQuery` filter just means "don't additionally restrict by project/environment/user" — apps are globally searchable (with post-query authorization filtering in `canUserViewApp`)

pr_title: DOM-75031 chore(Admin): Rework user admin services to support keycloak-only

comments.author: ddl-awroblicky
comments.body: I think targetUser is good but thoughts on renaming it to "idpUser"?

comments.author: ddl-martin-currao
comments.body:
_Aha! You fell into `userKeycloakHelper`'s trap._ It's not actually an IDP user.

`userKeycloakHelper.getDominoUserByKeycloakId` actually runs a `mergeUser` between the `maybeDominoUserId` and the actual `idpUser`, so the returned entity is none of them, but an updated version of any of them.

comments.author: ddl-awroblicky
comments.body: Can we call it mergedUser then?

comments.author: ddl-martin-currao
comments.body:
Hmmm. The issue is that later on it is actually further "merged" (kind of) with the result from the upgrade. 
Thus why it is called _target_, as it is whatever the target is, regardless of whether it's coming from a dominoUser or a keycloak user. Because if only the Keycloak user is present, it will be only an IDP user (without merging).

comments.author: ddl-awroblicky
comments.body: ah ok we can keep it as is then
